name: Fama
description: Fama, in Roman mythology, is the personification of rumor and gossip. She is often depicted as a winged goddess, swift and tireless in her dissemination of news, both true and false. Her origins are somewhat obscure, but she is generally considered to be a daughter of Earth (Terra) and is sometimes associated with Spes (Hope) or Metus (Fear), reflecting the duality of rumor's power to both inspire and terrify. Fama dwells in a house with countless windows, always open, allowing every whisper to escape and spread across the world. She has as many eyes, ears, and tongues as feathers on her body, enabling her to see, hear, and speak of everything. Her power lies in her ability to amplify and distort information, making it difficult to discern truth from falsehood. Fama's influence extends to all aspects of society, affecting reputations, inciting unrest, and influencing decisions. She is a capricious and unpredictable force, capable of both great good and great harm, and serves as a constant reminder of the power of words and the importance of skepticism. The Romans understood the volatile nature of rumors and recognized Fama as a potent force to be reckoned with. She is not typically worshipped with temples or sacrifices, but rather acknowledged and feared for her pervasive influence on human affairs. Her presence underscores the delicate balance between knowledge and misinformation, and the constant struggle to navigate the complex web of information that shapes our perceptions of the world.
subtitle: Goddess of Rumor and Gossip

keyMyths.title: Fama's Dwelling
keyMyths.description: Fama lives in a house with a thousand windows, always open, allowing every sound and whisper to escape. This symbolizes the uncontrollable spread of rumors.

keyMyths.title: The Spread of False News
keyMyths.description: Fama is often depicted as spreading false rumors, leading to chaos and conflict. This highlights the destructive potential of unchecked gossip.

keyMyths.title: Influence on Leaders
keyMyths.description: Fama's whispers can influence the decisions of powerful figures, demonstrating the impact of public opinion on governance.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: Provides a vivid description of Fama and her dwelling place.
sources.text: Provides a vivid description of Fama and her dwelling place.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: Details instances of Fama spreading misinformation and causing turmoil.
